title: Summarization in Production
concept: Deploying summarizers requires balancing document length constraints with generator latency. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Question Answering.'

exercises.prompt: When developers talk about 'latency' in AI apps, what do they mean?

options.text: The speed or time it takes to generate a response
The file size of the AI software

exercises.explain: Latency refers to the delay or time lag between sending a request to the AI and getting the response back.

cards.title: Speed vs. Size
cards.body: Have you ever waited forever for a webpage to load? When AI summarizes huge documents, it can take a long time to think. In the real world, developers have to balance how much text the AI reads with how fast it delivers the answer!

cards.title: What is Production Summarization?
cards.body: Deploying summarization models in the real world is a balancing act. You must fit long documents into the model's maximum size limit (context window) while making sure the AI generates the summary fast enough (low latency) so users aren't left waiting.

cards.title: The Mail Slot Dilemma
cards.body: Imagine trying to read a massive 500-page book through a tiny mail slot in your door. You can't squeeze the whole book through at once! Instead, you have to rip it into smaller pages and feed them through one by one, even though it takes longer. Similarly, a computer has a 'mail slot' size (context window) and a processing speed limit (latency) when reading long texts.

cards.title: Real-World Balance
cards.body: A news app wants to summarize articles instantly. If an article is too long, the app splits it into three parts, summarizes each part, and then combines them. This keeps the summary accurate without making the user stare at a loading spinner for too long.

cards.title: The 'Infinite Text' Myth
cards.body: People think that if you give an AI a massive document, it will instantly summarize it perfectly. In reality, sending too much text at once can crash the system, cost too much money, or make the AI respond too slowly for real-time apps.

cards.title: Speed vs. Context Size
cards.body: Choosing the right setup depends on your goals: speed or deep detail.

left.text: Fast replies, shorter inputs, keeps users happy and engaged.

right.text: Processes massive files, but takes much longer to generate results.

exercises.prompt: What happens if you try to send a document that is larger than the AI model's maximum limit?

options.text: The AI will automatically compress the text perfectly without any setup.
The system will encounter an error or cut off the text.
The AI will run faster because it has more information.
Nothing changes and the summary is generated instantly.

exercises.explain: If you feed too much text into a model at once, you will hit its memory limit (context window limit), causing the request to fail.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Summarizing a very long document generally takes more time (higher latency) than summarizing a short paragraph.
exercises.explain: Longer documents require the model to process more tokens, which increases the computation time and results in higher latency.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe the speed challenge in production AI.
exercises.sentence: When deploying a summarizer, we must balance document length with generator _, which is the time it takes the AI to reply.
exercises.explain: Latency measures the delay or waiting time. When summarizing documents for live users, keeping this wait time low is critical for a good experience.

exercises.prompt: Which of these shows a system optimized for low latency?

options.text: The AI creates a 10-page detailed report for every simple query.
The AI generates a short summary in under two seconds.
The AI takes five minutes to read a book and gets stuck loading.

exercises.explain: A summary that takes several minutes to load ruins the user experience. Good production design prioritizes fast response times.

exercises.prompt: You are launching an AI summarizer for massive 100-page medical journals. The AI has a maximum input limit and a strict speed budget. What is the best strategy to handle this in production?

options.text: Send the entire 50,000-word document as a single request to a model with a 2,000-word limit.
Make thousands of parallel requests simultaneously without checking the API rate limits.
Split the document into chunks that fit the model's limit, and run them through with rate-limit control.
Stop using AI models entirely and manually summarize the text by hand.

exercises.explain: Balancing chunk sizes, request rates, and model latency is key to deploying production summarization tools that users actually love. Next up: Question Answering.
